preview_text:
The model forecasts England as a strong favourite at 87%, leaving Ghana with a nominal 0% win probability and a 13% draw chance. This represents a decisive gap, though the underdog retains some theoretical path through a draw result.

The spread across outcomes reveals a match the model expects to lean heavily toward an England victory, with the draw as the only realistic alternative to that outcome. The win probabilities are far apart, suggesting limited competitive balance in the model's view. A draw remains plausible enough to mention, but a Ghana win registers as effectively impossible under these parameters.

The market consensus diverges notably from the model. Sportsbooks assign Ghana a 10% win probability and elevate the draw to 18%, while reducing England to 72%—a gap of roughly 15 percentage points on the favourite. Polymarket pricing sits between the two, closer to the books than the model. The dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment is more reliable; the discrepancy warrants attention from users building their own forecasts.
